nameMeanings.name: Galen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (Van Galen): habitational name, probably from the German town of Gahlen in North Rhine-Westphalia, or perhaps from the village Gaal in the province of North Brabant.
English (Cambridgeshire and Essex): variant of   Galyon   or   Gallon  .

variants.name: Galyon
meanings: English: variant of   Gallion  . This form of the surname has died out in Britain. Compare   Galyan   and   Galyen  .

nameMeanings.name: Jens
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Nielsen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Niels, Jorgen, Knud, Lars, Nels, Bent, Borge, Holger, Ove, Einer, Oluf.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Niels, a shortened form of Nikolaus (see Nicholas ). Compare Nielson , Neilsen , Nelsen , Nilsen , and Nilson .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Neilsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Ejnar, Gurli, Jorgen, Nels, Niels.
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Nielsen  . Compare   Neilson   3.

variants.name: Nelsen
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated in the Middle Ages as either a patronymic from   Neil   or a metronymic from   Nell .  Use as a given name probably began as a tribute to the British admiral  Lord Nelson  (  1758–1805 ), the victor of the Battle of Trafalgar;  see also  Horatio .  It is, however, now much more common in the United States than in Britain, its popularity there in the 1930s and 40s no doubt having been influenced by the American film actor and singer  Nelson Eddy  (  1901–67 ). Nowadays the name is universally associated with the South African statesman  Nelson Mandela  (  b. 1918 ).

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

historicEvents.title: The Hatuna Games
historicEvents.description: A conflict on September 29, 1306, between Birger, King of Sweden, and his brothers, the dukes Eric Magnusson and Valdemar Magnusson is known as The Hatuna Games. The brothers arrived to a party late in the night at King Birger's estate. The dukes captured and imprisoned Birger and his wife in Nykoping Castle's dungeon. Duke Eric then gained power over Sweden.

en.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen was born in Ellinge, Skåne, Sweden as the son of Niels Erlandsen and Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Christine Petersdatter. He died in 1320, in Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Denmark.

fr.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen est né à Ellinge, Skåne, Suède, fils de Niels Erlandsen et Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Christine Petersdatter. Il est décédé en 1320, à Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Danemark.

de.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen wurde in Ellinge, Skåne, Schweden, als Sohn von Niels Erlandsen und Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange geboren. Er hatte mit Christine Petersdatter mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1320 in Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Dänemark.

it.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen è nato il nel Ellinge, Skåne, Svezia come figlio di Niels Erlandsen e Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Christine Petersdatter. Morì nel 1320, nel Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Danimarca.

ja.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen GalenはスウェーデンスクaンエリンッジでNiels ErlandsenとElisabeth Pedersdatter Strangeの息子として生まれた。彼はChristine Petersdatterとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1320年にデンマークストルストルoムススaンaeスブイhオルムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen(은)는 스웨덴 스ka니 엘링에서 Niels Erlandsen(와)과 Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Christine Petersdatter(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1320년 덴마크 스토르스트o 수스a ae스비홀름에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen nasceu em Ellinge, Skåne, Suécia filho de Niels Erlandsen e Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Christine Petersdatter. Ele faleceu em 1320, em Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Dinamarca.

ru.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen родился в местности эллинг, скaн, Швеция. Его родителей звали Niels Erlandsen и Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Christine Petersdatter. Он умер в 1320 г. в местности нaeсбихолм, сусa, сторстрoм, Дания.

es.lifeSummary: Jens Nielsen Galen nació en Ellinge, Skåne, Suecia como hijo de Niels Erlandsen y Elisabeth Pedersdatter Strange. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Christine Petersdatter. Murió en 1320, en Næsbyholm, Suså, Storstrøm, Dinamarca.
